description:
You are an observability engineer who has just finished tuning a set of dashboards that rely on the Python package “prometheus-client”.  
    Before you check-in your work you want a permanent record of the exact version of that package that is installed on the system.

    Your goal
    ----------
    1. Query the locally-installed version of the Python package “prometheus-client”.  
    2. Persist the **full** package-information output in a log file so that a later automated audit can confirm the version.

    Exact requirements
    ------------------
    • You must accomplish the entire goal with **one single shell command** that:
         – invokes the standard Python user-level package manager (`python3 -m pip show prometheus-client`), and  
         – redirects that command’s complete, unmodified, multi-line stdout into the log file described below.  

      (A single command connected by operators such as `>` is still considered “one command” for this exercise.)

    • Create the directory /home/user/observability/reports if it does not already exist.  
      (The one-liner may create the directory implicitly with suitable redirection tricks if you wish, but no extra commands are allowed.)

    • The resulting log file must be located at the exact path:
         /home/user/observability/reports/prometheus_client_version.log

    • The checker will open that file and look for **verbatim lines** of the canonical `pip show` output, in particular:
         Name: prometheus-client  
         Version: <SOME_VERSION_STRING>

      Therefore do not trim, re-format or add any other content.

    • No other files, directories or console output format are evaluated.

    Hint (do not paste this as part of your answer):
    ------------------------------------------------
    A correct one-liner typically takes the form  
       python3 -m pip show prometheus-client > /home/user/observability/reports/prometheus_client_version.log
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth information used by the automated grader.
        The grader will first ensure that the directory
        /home/user/observability/reports/ exists (creating it if necessary) and
        that the Python package "prometheus-client" version 0.19.0 is already
        present in the execution environment.

        After the agent finishes, the grader will read the file
        /home/user/observability/reports/prometheus_client_version.log
        and verify that:

        1. It exists and is readable by the "user" account.
        2. It contains at least these two exact lines (case-sensitive):
              Name: prometheus-client
              Version: 0.19.0
           in the canonical order produced by `pip show`.
        3. No additional manipulation (e.g., JSON, quotation marks, headers)
           precedes or follows the standard `pip show` output.
    -->
name: task_000000_109992d1
